title: ranunculus aquatilis var. porteri
intro: Ranunculus aquatilis var. porteri, also known as the western water crowfoot, is a perennial aquatic plant native to North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to its saponin and alkaloid content. The health benefits of Ranunculus aquatilis var. porteri are said to include its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with digestive issues and skin conditions. The plant can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, although its use should be approached with caution due to its potential toxicity.
